chunks.text: C.P.No.D-291 of 2022 Page 1 of 2 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P.No. D- 291 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. FRESH CASE 1.For orders on CMA 1228/22 2.For orders on office objection 3.For orders on CMA 1229/22 4.For hearing of main case. 5.For orders on CMA 1230/22 22-03-2022 Mr. Abdul Naeem Pirzada, Advocate along with Abdul Hafeez Bhutto, Petitioner. ***** 1. Granted. 2-5 Through this Petition, the Petitioner has sought for the following relief(s): “a. To direct the respondents to allocate funds for purchasing of new biometric machine or directions to use any other head of account to purchase the same. b. To direct the respondents to act in accordance with law and not to take any adverse action against the petitioner without observing proper procedure, principles of natural justice and the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. c. To restrain the respondents from taking any action detrimental to the legal and fundamental rights of the petitioner, more particularly against the service till final decision of the present petition”. We have confronted the Petitioner’s Counsel as to the maintainability of this Petition, as admittedly the Petitioner is Principal, Government Degree College, Daharki and cannot be an aggrieved person and how this Petition is maintainable and could be filed by a Government Servant against his own Department / Superiors regarding alleged hindrance in discharge of his official responsibilities. Learned Counsel has not been able to satisfy as to how this petition can be maintained; however, has made a request to withdraw the same.

We have confronted the Petitioner’s Counsel as to the maintainability of this Petition, as admittedly the Petitioner is Principal, Government Degree College, Daharki and cannot be an aggrieved person and how this Petition is maintainable and could be filed by a Government Servant against his own Department / Superiors regarding alleged hindrance in discharge of his official responsibilities. Learned Counsel has not been able to satisfy as to how this petition can be maintained; however, has made a request to withdraw the same. C.P.No.D-291 of 2022 Page 2 of 2 Only as an indulgence, the said request is acceded to and the petition is dismissed as withdrawn; however, the petitioner is warned to be careful in future before filing such frivolous petitions, failing which we would be compelled to refer his case for proceedings of misconduct within the contemplation of Rule 21 of The Sindh Civil Servants (Efficiency & Discipline) Rules, 1973, read with Rule 192 and 303 of The Sindh Civil Servants (Conduct) Rules 2008. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
